children.heading: .—Public-private Partnerships
children.label: a .—Public-private Partnerships
children.text: The Secretary of Defense may enter into arrangements known as public-private partnerships with appropriate entities outside the Government for the purposes of facilitating the activities of the designated Defense Agency. The Secretary may only partner with foreign governments or foreign entities with the concurrence of the Secretary of State. Any such arrangement shall be entered into in accordance with authorities provided under this section or any other authority otherwise available to the Secretary. Regulations prescribed under subsection (f)(1) shall include provisions for the establishment and implementation of such partnerships. An employee of an entity outside the Government that has entered into a public-private partnership, cooperative agreement, or a grant arrangement with, or in direct support of, the designated Defense Agency under this section shall be considered to be an employee of the Federal Government by reason of participation in such partnership, cooperative agreement, or grant, only for the purposes of (relating to maintenance of records on individuals).section 552a of title 5

children.heading: .—Acceptance of Voluntary Personal Services
children.label: b .—Acceptance of Voluntary Personal Services
children.text: The Secretary of Defense may accept voluntary services to facilitate accounting for missing persons in the same manner as the Secretary of a military department may accept such services under .section 1588(a)(9) of this title

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary of Defense may enter into a cooperative agreement with, or make a grant to, a private entity for purposes related to support of the activities of the designated Defense Agency.

children.heading: .—Inapplicability of certain contract requirements
children.label: 2 .—Inapplicability of certain contract requirements
children.text: Notwithstanding , the Secretary may enter such cooperative agreements or grants on a sole-source basis pursuant to .section 3201(e) of this titlesection 3204(a)(5) of this title

children.heading: .—Use of Department of Defense Personal Property
children.label: d .—Use of Department of Defense Personal Property
children.text: The Secretary may allow a private entity to use, at no cost, personal property of the Department of Defense to assist the entity in supporting the activities of the designated Defense Agency.

children.heading: .—Authority to accept
children.label: 1 .—Authority to accept
children.text: Subject to subsection (f)(2), the Secretary may accept, hold, administer, spend, and use any gift of personal property, money, or services made on the condition that the gift be used for the purpose of facilitating accounting for missing persons pursuant to .section 1501(a)(2)(C) of this title

children.heading: .—Gift funds
children.label: 2 .—Gift funds
children.text: Gifts and bequests of money accepted under this subsection shall be deposited in the Treasury in the Department of Defense General Gift Fund.

children.heading: .—Use of gifts
children.label: 3 .—Use of gifts
children.text: Personal property and money accepted under this subsection may be used by the Secretary, and services accepted under this subsection may be performed, without further specific authorization in law.

children.heading: .—Expenses of transfer
children.label: 4 .—Expenses of transfer
children.text: The Secretary may pay all necessary expenses in connection with the conveyance or transfer of a gift accepted under this subsection.

children.heading: .—Expenses of care
children.label: 5 .—Expenses of care
children.text: The Secretary may pay all reasonable and necessary expenses in connection with the care of a gift accepted under this subsection.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary of Defense shall prescribe regulations to implement this section.

children.heading: .—Limitation
children.label: 2 .—Limitation
children.text: Such regulations shall provide that acceptance of a gift (including a gift of services) or use of a gift under this section may not occur if the nature or circumstances of the acceptance or use would compromise the integrity, or the appearance of integrity, of any program of the Department of Defense or any individual involved in such program.

children.heading: .—Cooperative agreement
children.label: 1 .—Cooperative agreement
children.text: The term “cooperative agreement” means an authorized cooperative agreement as described in .section 6305 of title 31

children.heading: .—Grant
children.label: 2 .—Grant
children.text: The term “grant” means an authorized grant as described in .section 6304 of title 31

children.heading: .—Gift
children.label: 3 .—Gift
children.text: The term “gift” includes a devise or bequest.
